titleEn: Why variable-rate application still struggles to gain traction
titleZh: 变量施肥技术为何难以普及
summaryZh: 本文探讨了变量施肥技术（VRA）在荷兰大田作物农场中推广缓慢的原因。尽管该技术已存在多年，但实际采用率不足10%。采访显示，障碍并非单纯技术问题，而是涉及农场经济、地块特征、软件复杂性以及不确定的投资回报。文章为智慧农业从业者提供了关于技术落地瓶颈的深度洞察。
featuredReason: 本文揭示了精准农业技术（如变量施肥）在实际推广中的核心痛点，对智慧畜牧行业人士理解技术落地的经济与操作障碍具有重要参考价值。
contentFull: Why variable-rate application still struggles to gain traction 16-07 | Smart farming | ArticleLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintIn an estimated 10% of cases, fertiliser is spread using a task map. This is often reinforced by the eco-scheme. Photo: Michel VeldermanVariable-rate application has been available for years, yet adoption remains limited on many arable farms. Interviews with machinery manufacturers, precision farming specialists and crop advisers show that the barriers are rarely technical alone. Farm economics, field characteristics, software complexity and uncertain returns continue to slow the uptake of prescription maps for seeding and fertiliser application. Fewer than 10% of Dutch arable farmers/growers, contractors and livestock farmersLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrint René Koerhuis Precision Farming SpecialistMore abouttask mapsadLEARN MORERelated articlesPremium10-10-2024 | ArticleVariable seeding with task maps is not yet common practice Buyers guides & Catalogues Subscribe to our YouTube channel Taiwan Smart Agriweek 2026Sep 8-10, 2026 TaiNEX 1, Tapei
translationZh:
变量施肥技术已问世多年，但在许多大田作物农场中，其采用率仍然有限。对农机厂商、精准农业专家和作物顾问的采访表明，障碍很少仅仅是技术层面的。农场经济状况、地块特征、软件复杂性以及不确定的回报，持续拖慢播种和施肥处方图的应用步伐。

在荷兰，使用任务地图进行施肥的案例估计仅占10%。这通常受到生态计划的推动。

荷兰大田作物种植者/农场主、承包商和畜牧养殖者中，采用该技术的比例不足10%。

精准农业专家 René Koerhuis 指出，变量施肥的推广仍面临多重现实挑战。
author: René Koerhuis
contentHtml: Variable-rate application has been available for years, yet adoption remains limited on many arable farms. Interviews with machinery manufacturers, precision farming specialists and crop advisers show that the barriers are rarely technical alone. Farm economics, field characteristics, software complexity and uncertain returns continue to slow the uptake of prescription maps for seeding and fertiliser application. […]
